title: Study to Describe the Safety, Tolerability, Immunogenicity, and Efficacy of RNA Vaccine Candidates Against COVID-19 in Healthy Individuals
summary: This document details a completed Phase 1/2/3 randomized, placebo-controlled, observer-blind study (NCT04368728) evaluating the safety, tolerability, immunogenicity, and efficacy of SARS-CoV-2 RNA vaccine candidates, specifically BNT162b2, against COVID-19 in healthy individuals across various age groups and dose regimens, including booster doses and variant-specific candidates.

children.title: Study Details Tab
children.summary: This section provides an initial overview of the study, outlining its primary objective to evaluate vaccine candidates and details of its multi-phase design and target population.

children.title: Study Overview
children.summary: An overview of the Phase 1/2/3 randomized, placebo-controlled, observer-blind, dose-finding study design, outlining its aims to identify vaccine candidates and evaluate safety, tolerability, immunogenicity, and efficacy.

children.title: Brief Summary
children.summary: This section describes the study as a Phase 1/2/3, randomized, placebo-controlled trial to evaluate RNA vaccine candidates against COVID-19, including dose-finding, efficacy evaluation for BNT162b2, and boostability assessments across different age groups.

children.title: Official Title
children.summary: The official title describes the study as a Phase 1/2/3, placebo-controlled, randomized, observer-blind, dose-finding study evaluating the safety, tolerability, immunogenicity, and efficacy of SARS-CoV-2 RNA vaccine candidates in healthy individuals.

children.title: Conditions
children.summary: This section lists the medical conditions relevant to the study, specifically SARS-CoV-2 Infection and COVID-19.

children.title: Intervention / Treatment
children.summary: Details the biological interventions used in the study, including vaccine candidates BNT162b1, BNT162b2, BNT162b2SA, and placebo.

children.title: Other Study ID Numbers
children.summary: Provides additional identifiers for the study, such as C4591001 and the EudraCT Number 2020-002641-42, along with key study dates and enrollment figures.

children.title: Contacts and Locations
children.summary: This section provides contact details for study inquiries and general information on how to interpret study record location sections.

children.title: Participation Criteria
children.summary: Outlines the general guidelines researchers use to select participants, emphasizing the importance of eligibility criteria for clinical research.

children.title: Eligibility Criteria
children.summary: Detailed criteria for study inclusion and exclusion, covering participant demographics, health status, medical history, and prior treatments relevant to the vaccine study.

children.title: Description
children.summary: Provides a detailed breakdown of both the specific conditions and characteristics required for participants to be included in the study and those that would lead to their exclusion.

children.title: Inclusion Criteria:
children.summary: Lists the specific requirements participants must meet to be enrolled in the study, including age ranges, willingness to comply with procedures, and being healthy or at risk for COVID-19.

children.title: Exclusion Criteria:
children.summary: Details conditions or characteristics that prevent individuals from participating in the study, such as specific medical conditions, prior infections, or receiving certain treatments.

children.title: Ages Eligible for Study
children.summary: Specifies that participants eligible for the study must be 12 years and older, encompassing child, adult, and older adult age groups.

children.title: Sexes Eligible for Study
children.summary: Indicates that participants of all sexes are eligible to enroll in the study.

children.title: Study Plan
children.summary: Provides comprehensive details on the study's design, including how interventions are structured, what outcomes are measured, key collaborators, and relevant publications.

children.title: How is the study designed?
children.summary: Describes the fundamental aspects of the study design, including primary purpose, allocation, interventional model, and masking, along with information on arms and interventions.

children.title: Design Details
children.summary: Outlines the specific design elements of the study, such as its primary purpose of prevention, randomized allocation, parallel assignment model, and triple masking (participant, care provider, investigator).

children.title: Arms and Interventions
children.summary: Details the different treatment arms and interventions used in the study, providing links to tables with specific information.

children.title: What is the study measuring?
children.summary: Explains the primary and secondary outcome measures used to assess the vaccine candidates' safety, immunogenicity, and efficacy.

children.title: Primary Outcome Measures
children.summary: Lists the main objectives and endpoints the study is designed to measure, supported by links to detailed table data.

children.title: Secondary Outcome Measures
children.summary: Outlines additional endpoints and observations the study is designed to evaluate, supplementing the primary efficacy and safety assessments, supported by links to detailed table data.

children.title: Collaborators and Investigators
children.summary: Identifies the sponsor (BioNTech SE), collaborators (Pfizer), and study director (Pfizer CT.gov Call Center) involved in the research.

children.title: Publications
children.summary: Lists scientific and medical articles from PubMed that are related to the study or vaccine research in general, automatically populated from the public database.

children.title: Study Record Dates
children.summary: Tracks the submission and update history of the study record and summary results on ClinicalTrials.gov, ensuring adherence to quality control standards.

children.title: Study Registration Dates
children.summary: Provides information on the dates related to the initial registration of the study on ClinicalTrials.gov.

children.title: Study Record Updates
children.summary: Details the chronology of updates made to the study record on ClinicalTrials.gov, reflecting any changes or new information.

children.title: More Information
children.summary: Offers additional resources and details related to the study, including keywords, MeSH terms, data sharing plans, regulatory information, and helpful external links.

children.title: Terms related to this study
children.summary: Provides keywords from BioNTech SE and relevant Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) to categorize the study's focus and content.

children.title: Keywords Provided by BioNTech SE
children.summary: Lists keywords used by the sponsor, BioNTech SE, to describe the study, such as COVID-19, Coronavirus, Vaccine, SARS-CoV-2, and RNA Vaccine.

children.title: Additional Relevant MeSH Terms
children.summary: Includes a comprehensive list of Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) that are relevant to the study's topics, providing standardized indexing for clinical research.

children.title: Plan for Individual Participant Data (IPD)
children.summary: States whether there is a plan to share individual participant data from the study (indicated as 'No').

children.title: Drug and device information, study documents, and helpful links
children.summary: Provides information on whether the study involves a U.S. FDA-regulated drug product and includes helpful external links provided by BioNTech SE for further information.
